today_news.title: The Download: how AI is used for military targeting, and the Pentagon’s war on Claude
today_news.summary: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. today_news.title: Why physical AI is becoming manufacturing’s next advantage
today_news.summary: For decades, manufacturers have pursued automation to drive efficiency, reduce costs, and stabilize operations. That approach delivered meaningful gains, but it is no longer enough. Today’s manufacturing leaders face a different challenge: how to grow amid labor constraints, rising complexity, and i...

today_news.title: Future AI chips could be built on glass
today_news.summary: Human-made glass is thousands of years old. But it’s now poised to find its way into the AI chips used in the world’s newest and largest data centers. This year, a South Korean company called Absolics is planning to start commercial production of special glass panels designed to make next-generation...

today_news.title: Elon Musk admits xAI "was not built right first time around," launches full restructuring
today_news.summary: Elon Musk's AI company xAI is going through a major restructuring. Musk acknowledged on X that the company "was not built right first time around" and is now being rebuilt from the foundations up.

today_news.title: Google explains the differences between its three Nano Banana image generation models
today_news.summary: A new guide from Google breaks down the three Nano Banana image models and when to use each one. The cheaper Nano Banana 2 reportedly delivers 95 percent of Pro's capabilities and can search the web for reference images on its own before generating output.

today_news.title: Anthropic drops the surcharge for million-token context windows, making Opus 4.6 and Sonnet 4.6 far cheaper
today_news.summary: Anthropic removes the surcharge for long contexts in Claude Opus 4.6 and Sonnet 4.6: requests with more than 200,000 tokens no longer cost up to twice as much.

today_news.title: AI chips are pushing everything else off TSMC's most advanced production lines
today_news.summary: By 2027, 86 percent of TSMC's N3 capacity could go to AI accelerators, according to SemiAnalysis. Smartphones are becoming a buffer for overflow demand.

today_news.title: Ukraine opens its battlefield data to allies to train AI models for autonomous drones
today_news.summary: Ukraine opens its battlefield data to allies to train AI models for autonomous drones.
